diagrams.title: Software Quality Metrics Categories
diagrams.description: A hierarchical diagram showing the three main categories of software quality metrics: Product Metrics (quality of the software itself), Process Metrics (quality of the development process), and Project Metrics (quality of project management). Each category breaks down into specific measurable attributes with examples.
diagrams.purpose: To help learners understand the different types of quality metrics and their classification, which is fundamental to measuring and improving software quality

diagrams.title: ISO 25010 Quality Characteristics Model
diagrams.description: A comprehensive diagram illustrating the ISO 25010 standard for software product quality, showing the eight main quality characteristics organized in a structured layout. Each characteristic branches to its sub-characteristics, providing a complete framework for evaluating software quality.
diagrams.purpose: To provide a standardized framework for understanding and measuring software quality characteristics, enabling consistent quality evaluation across different software systems

diagrams.title: Metrics Measurement Process Flow
diagrams.description: A cyclic flowchart diagram showing the continuous process of software quality measurement and improvement. It starts from requirements and flows through development phases, data collection, metric calculation, analysis, reporting, and feedback for process improvement.
diagrams.purpose: To illustrate the continuous cycle of quality measurement and improvement, helping practitioners understand how to implement effective quality monitoring and use metrics for decision-making in software projects
